title: Final 3-Month Mega Review
learningGoal: The learner will review and synthesize the major grammatical structures from the entire 3-month curriculum, from basic particles to advanced Keigo and Causative-Passive forms.
instructions: Welcome to the final block of your 3-month Japanese journey! This lesson is a high-level review of the most critical grammatical transformations you have learned.

lessonContent.bodyText: Remember the foundation. Particles mark the grammatical role of words. 'Wa' (Topic), 'Ga' (Subject), 'Wo' (Direct Object), 'Ni' (Time/Destination), 'De' (Location of action / Tool).

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: わたしは はしで すしを たべます。
targetLanguageExamples.transliteration: Watashi wa hashi de sushi wo tabemasu.
targetLanguageExamples.baseTranslation: I eat sushi with chopsticks. (Tool 'de', Object 'wo').

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: にほんに いきます。

lessonContent.sectionTitle: Month 2 Review: The Te-Form & Plain Forms
lessonContent.bodyText: The Te-form allowed you to connect sentences, make requests, and ask for permission. Plain forms allowed you to speak casually and create relative clauses.

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: しゃしんを とっても いいですか。
targetLanguageExamples.transliteration: Shashin wo totte mo ii desu ka.
targetLanguageExamples.baseTranslation: May I take a picture? (Te-form + mo ii desu ka).

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: あした いく ひと。
targetLanguageExamples.baseTranslation: The person who will go tomorrow. (Plain form relative clause).

lessonContent.bodyText: Month 3 introduced nuance: Potential (can do), Volitional (let's do), Passive (was done to), Causative (make do), and Causative-Passive (forced to do).

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: にほんごを はなせます。
ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。
targetLanguageExamples.baseTranslation: I was forced to do overtime by the boss. (Causative-Passive).

lessonContent.bodyText: The ultimate test of Japanese business fluency: knowing when to elevate the listener (Sonkeigo) and when to lower yourself (Kenjougo).

targetLanguageExamples.targetText: しゃちょうは いらっしゃいます。
わたくしが まいります。

learningObjective: The learner will be able to identify and differentiate between the 10 major verb conjugations taught over the 3-month period.

examples.targetText: のむ、のんで、のめる、のもう、のまれる、のませる、のまされる。
examples.transliteration: Nomu, Nonde, Nomeru, Nomou, Nomareru, Nomaseru, Nomasareru.
examples.baseTranslation: Drink, Drink (Te), Can drink, Let's drink, Was drunk/affected, Make drink, Forced to drink.
examples.explanation: This is the evolution of a single verb (Nomu) across the curriculum.

importantRules.ruleText: Verb groups matter. Always remember if a verb is an Ru-verb (Taberu) or an U-verb (Nomu, Iku).
importantRules.exceptionNotes: Irregular verbs Suru (to do) and Kuru (to come) must be memorized separately for every form.

cultureContext.contextText: Completing this curriculum means you have encountered almost all the grammar required for JLPT N4, and some concepts from N3. You have the theoretical knowledge to survive and thrive in Japan.
dosList: Review your flashcards. Language requires constant upkeep.
dontsList: Do not worry if you make mistakes with Keigo or Causative forms in real life. Native speakers know it is difficult for learners.

summary: You have mastered Particles, Te-forms, Plain forms, Conditionals, Potential, Volitional, Passives, Causatives, and Keigo.

checkpointQuestions.questionText: What form is 'Katte mo ii desu ka'?
checkpointQuestions.expectedAnswer: Te-form request for permission (May I buy?).

checkpointQuestions.questionText: If you are going to a meeting, do you use 'Irassharu' or 'Mairu'?

keyTakeaways: Verb forms dictate intention, ability, and social dynamic.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.conceptSummary: A comprehensive review of 3 months of Japanese grammar.
importantFacts: U-verbs have complex conjugations across the A, I, U, E, O vowel sounds.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.expectedUnderstanding: Learner is prepared for the final comprehensive assessment.

feedbackRules.onPass: Congratulations on reaching the final review!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: わたしは はしで すしを たべます。にほんに いきます。しゃしんを とっても いいですか。あした いく ひと。にほんごを はなせます。ぶちょうに ざんぎょうを させられました。しゃちょうは いらっしゃいます。わたくしが まいります。のむ、のんで、のめる、のもう、のまれる、のませる、のまされる。
